# depends.cc

depends.cc is a dependency state tracking service. It models systems as directed acyclic graphs (DAGs) where each node has a traffic-light state: green, yellow, or red.

The service is a passive receiver: it does not poll, crawl, or run scheduled health checks. State arrives via the API or CLI; the only routine outbound HTTP from the server is delivery (webhooks, email). To probe URLs on a schedule, define `meta.checks` and run `depends check` from your own cron (or equivalent).

When a node's state changes, the "effective state" of all downstream dependents is automatically recomputed. If a database goes red, every service that depends on it becomes effectively red — without updating each one individually.

## Key concepts

- **Node**: A thing with a state (green/yellow/red). Has an optional label, reason, and solution.
- **Edge**: A dependency between nodes. "A depends on B" means A's effective state is affected by B.
- **Effective state**: The worst state of a node and all its transitive dependencies (red > yellow > green).
- **Namespace**: A named container for a graph of nodes and edges. Owned by a token.
- **Token**: An authentication credential. Prefix: `dep_`. Owns one or more namespaces.
- **Reason**: Why a node is in its current state (e.g. "disk full on /var/data").
- **Solution**: What to do about it (e.g. "Clear /var/log, expand volume").
- **default_state**: Initial state for new nodes (default: yellow). Set to green for aggregator nodes that only reflect their dependencies.
- **TTL**: Optional per-node duration. If set, a currently green node with no state write within the TTL resolves to yellow (stale) when read. Evaluation is lazy — run `depends check` on a schedule or any state write if you need timely TTL transitions.
- **Notifications**: Rules in the graph (`depends.yml` or API) watch nodes and send webhooks and/or email when effective state matches `on` (`watch`, `on`, optional `ack` for suppress-until-ack).

## meta.checks and depends check

In `depends.yml`, optional `meta.checks` is a list of probes: each has `url` and `grep` (a string or list of strings that must appear in the HTTP response body). `depends check` GETs each URL, requires HTTP 200 and all greps to match, then PUTs the node to green or red. Use `--dry-run` to print results without updating state.

Docs (HTML): https://depends.cc/docs#health-checks

## CLI quick reference

```
depends serve [-p <port>]              Run the server locally (default: 3000)
depends signup <email> <lak_...>       Hosted signup: Legendum account key; token emailed
depends init                           Create a depends.yml in the current directory
depends push [--prune]                 Upload depends.yml (auto-creates namespace)
depends pull                           Download remote graph as depends.yml
depends show                           Print current spec (YAML) without saving
depends status [<ns/node>]             Show states (add --json for machine output)
depends set [<ns>/]<node> <state>      Set green, yellow, or red
depends graph                          Print dependency tree
depends events [<ns/node>]             Recent state changes (--json, --limit)
depends validate                       Validate depends.yml (errors, cycles)
depends check [--dry-run]              Run meta.checks and update state (cron)
depends diff                           Show what would change on push
depends delete                         Delete a namespace and all its data
depends usage                          Usage stats for current billing period
depends update                         Update CLI to latest version
depends admin tokens                   List tokens (server admin)
depends admin plan <email> [plan]      Show or set plan (server admin)
```

## API quick reference

Base URL: https://depends.cc/v1 (or http://localhost:3000/v1 for local)
Auth: Bearer token in Authorization header

POST   /v1/signup                              Body: {"email","account_key"} — Legendum `lak_...` key; token emailed
POST   /v1/namespaces                          Create namespace (token auth)
DELETE /v1/namespaces/:ns                     Delete namespace
GET    /v1/nodes/:ns                          List nodes
GET    /v1/nodes/:ns/:id                      Get node detail
PUT    /v1/nodes/:ns/:id                      Create/update node (label, depends_on, meta, ttl, …)
DELETE /v1/nodes/:ns/:id                     Delete node
PUT    /v1/state/:ns/:id/:state              Set state (green/yellow/red)
GET    /v1/events/:ns[/:id]                  List events; query: limit, since, until
GET    /v1/graph/:ns                         Get graph (?format=yaml)
PUT    /v1/graph/:ns                         Upload YAML graph (?prune=true removes unlisted nodes)
GET    /v1/graph/:ns/:id                     Subgraph for one node
GET    /v1/graph/:ns/:id/upstream            Transitive dependencies
GET    /v1/graph/:ns/:id/downstream         Transitive dependents
GET    /v1/notifications/:ns                 List notification rules
PUT    /v1/notifications/:ns                 Create/update rule
DELETE /v1/notifications/:ns/:ruleId         Delete rule
POST   /v1/notifications/:ns/:ruleId/ack     Acknowledge / un-suppress rule
GET    /v1/usage/:ns                         Usage stats and limits

## Setting state with context

PUT /v1/state/:ns/:id/red
Headers:
  X-Reason: disk full on /var/data
  X-Solution: Clear /var/log, expand volume

## Local mode

If no token is configured, the CLI defaults to local mode:
- Token: dep_local (well-known, no signup needed)
- URL: http://localhost:3000/v1
- No limits (enterprise plan)
- Start server: depends serve

## Status page

GET /ns/:ns              All nodes as plain text (HTTP Basic Auth: any username, token as password)
GET /ns/:ns.json         All nodes as JSON (same as GET /v1/nodes/:ns)
GET /ns/:ns.yaml         Full graph as YAML (same format as depends.yml)
GET /ns/:ns/:node        Single node as plain text
GET /ns/:ns/:node.json   Single node as JSON (same as GET /v1/nodes/:ns/:node)

## Machine-readable docs

GET /docs with `Accept: application/json` returns structured API documentation (`DOCS_JSON`).
CLI: `depends status --json` and `depends events --json` for machine-readable output (not a query flag on GET /v1/nodes).
